headlines.description: Late goals from Bruno Fernandes and Manuel Ugarte helped Manchester United come from behind to earn a dramatic 2-2 draw at Everton in the Premier League on Saturday after the hosts were denied a stoppage-time penalty following a VAR review.
headlines.shortLinkText: United rally to draw at Everton after pen drama

headlines.description: Matheus Cunha's 14th Premier League goal of the season earned Wolverhampton Wanderers a 1-0 win over 10-man Bournemouth on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Cunha's goal gives Wolves win over Bournemouth

headlines.description: Arsenal's Premier League title hopes suffered a hammer blow as Jarrod Bowen's goal just before halftime gave London rivals West Ham United a surprise 1-0 away victory on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Arsenal title hopes in tatters after West Ham loss

headlines.description: A first-half own goal by Fulham defender Joachim Andersen and a second-half rocket from Daniel Munoz gave Crystal Palace a 2-0 Premier League win away to the Cottagers on Saturday, with the home side failing to register a shot on target.
headlines.shortLinkText: Palace beat Fulham for fourth straight away win

headlines.description: Tottenham Hotspur winger Brennan Johnson scored twice in the first half before goals from Djed Spence and Dejan Kulusevski wrapped up a 4-1 win at Ipswich Town.
headlines.shortLinkText: Spurs' revival continues as they outclass Ipswich

headlines.description: Brighton & Hove Albion added to relegation-threatened Southampton's woes with a 4-0 win at the St Mary's Stadium on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Brighton pile misery on Saints with 4-0 thrashing

headlines.description: Aston Villa's Marco Asensio bagged a brace and fellow newcomer Marcus Rashford had two assists as their side roared back from a goal down to beat Chelsea 2-1.
headlines.shortLinkText: Rashford, Asensio inspire Villa to win over Chelsea
